rationale: Zhu and Ratner (2015) is not just listed in background citations; it is explicitly discussed in Study 2a as a competing mechanism for the focal effect. The authors operationalize this by measuring arousal and mood with items attributed to Zhu and Ratner and by including arousal in mediation analyses. They then report null support for this pathway, so the citation functions as a meaningful design and interpretive reference rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Zhu and Ratner (2015) as a theory-based alternative account, drawing on its claim that arousal can polarize preferences and reduce variety seeking, and borrows its arousal and mood measures. It tests this pathway in the rejection-versus-choice context and finds it does not explain the focal effect.
